body.u: When I look back, I remember the day I left school. I was sixteen at the time, and at the threshold of my life. I had everything to look forward to. I had a ‘decent’ job, working in Boots. I enjoyed the job very much, and I was quite happy for two years.
During those two years, I went regularly to night school doing shorthand typing and taking O’level English.
I passed the English, and got good speeds in shortandshorthand and typing.
I started applying for secretarial jobs, and eventually got one.
I was settled in my job, and remained there for a long time.
I was about 32, when I met up with my old school friend, she was married with four children. She didntdidn't work, and she would say how lucky I was to be unmarried and have a decent job.
I started seeing her again quite regularly, and met her brother.

It wasn’t love at first sight, but we both had an immediate liking to each other. I began to see more and more of him, and realised that I a strong feeling for him and he realised that he had a strong feeling for me.
He proposed to me, whilst we were having dinner. I said ‘yes’ straight away. We were married 2 months later, I kept my job as a secretary, and he as an electrical
fitter. He was the best in the trade. We had our first child, when I was 33. I decided to pack my job in, and become a mother and housewife.
Our son was about 2 when I became pregnant, again, but I lost it after 3 months.
We decided to not have any more children. When our son Mark, was about 13, his father was tragically killed in a car crash.
We were both heart broken, but we pulled ourselves together, and I got another job.
I wouldn’t say Mark and I were rich, but we were quite well off. Mark was studying for his A’levels then hoping to go on to university, to become a doctor. I helped him in everywayevery way I could.

Mark got married when he was 26. His wedding day, was the happiest of my life. He had a beautiful wife. It wasn’t long before she herself, had a child. She was a bouncing beauty, the image of her mother.
I idolised her, and still do really. Well! Wouldn’t you, if she was your only grandchild?
